course_title: Physical Activity and Sport Leadership in Schools

learning_outcomes.description: APST 3.3: Use teaching strategies: Include a range of teaching strategies.
APST 3.4: Select and use resources: Demonstrate knowledge of a range of resources, including ICT, that engage students in their learning.
APST 3.5: Use effective classroom communication: Demonstrate a range of verbal and nonverbal communication strategies to support student engagement.
APST 3.6: Evaluate and improve teaching programs: Demonstrate broad knowledge of strategies that can be used to evaluate teaching programs to improve student learning.
APST 4.2: Manage classroom activities: Demonstrate the capacity to organise classroom activities and provide clear directions.
APST 6.1: Identify and plan professional learning needs: Demonstrate an understanding of the role of the National Professional Standards for Teachers in identifying professional learning needs.
APST 6.2: Engage in professional learning and improve practice.
APST 6.3: Engage with colleagues and improve practice: Seek and apply constructive feedback from supervisors and teachers to improve teaching practices.

APST: 6.4 Apply professional learning and improve student learning. Demonstrate an understanding of the rationale for continued professional learning and the implications for improved student learning.

APST 7.4: Engage with professional teaching networks and broader communities: Understand the role of external professionals and community representatives in broadening teachers professional knowledge and practice.
Demonstrate the ability to effectively collaborate with others to complete group tasks.

assessments.task_description:

Individual task.

Full description in Blackboard.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 4(27 Jul - 23 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Leadership Identity & Leading Others

This series explores the qualities of effective leadership. It supports you to identify and develop your own leadership identity by examining your values, leadership style and your school's strategic goals. You will also build your understanding to create and lead effective teams, including how to empower students to grow as capable and confident leaders. This series draws on both mainstream and First Nations Australian leadership perspectives.


Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership Identity & Leading Others This series explores the qualities of effective leadership. It supports you to identify and develop your own leadership identity by examining your values, leadership style and your school's strategic goals. You will also build your understanding to create and lead effective teams, including how to empower students to grow as capable and confident leaders. This series draws on both mainstream and First Nations Australian leadership perspectives.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Managing Sport & Physical Activity Events in Schools

This ongoing series focuses on developing skills in planning, organising and managing school-based sport and physical activity events. It integrates the Sport Education model and Fair Play pedagogies to promote meaningful leadership, teamwork, engagement and particpation in sport and physical activity contexts. Students will plan, lead, and participate in a engaging, inclusive and competitive tournament-style sporting event that showcases teamwork, organisation and positive sporting behaviours. This tournament serves as the culminating experience of EDUC2011.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L07, L08, L09, L11
learning_activities.topic_title: Managing Sport & Physical Activity Events in Schools
learning_activities.topic_description: Managing Sport & Physical Activity Events in Schools This ongoing series focuses on developing skills in planning, organising and managing school-based sport and physical activity events. It integrates the Sport Education model and Fair Play pedagogies to promote meaningful leadership, teamwork, engagement and particpation in sport and physical activity contexts. Students will plan, lead, and participate in a engaging, inclusive and competitive tournament-style sporting event that showcases teamwork, organisation and positive sporting behaviours. This tournament serves as the culminating experience of EDUC2011.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 6(24 Aug - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Outsourcing & External Providers

This series examines how you can evaluate the value of external physical activity and wellbeing providers to determine whether they enhance the educational outcomes of your students. It supports decision-making by identifying gaps in programs and staff expertise, and by assessing alignment with school goals. It also builds your capacity to make informed decisions and confidently advocate for or against the use of external providers, as well as for staff professional development to strengthen practice and outcomes.


Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L06, L07, L08, L09, L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Outsourcing & External Providers This series examines how you can evaluate the value of external physical activity and wellbeing providers to determine whether they enhance the educational outcomes of your students. It supports decision-making by identifying gaps in programs and staff expertise, and by assessing alignment with school goals. It also builds your capacity to make informed decisions and confidently advocate for or against the use of external providers, as well as for staff professional development to strengthen practice and outcomes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 10(07 Sep - 11 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Program Design & Design Thinking

This series introduces the Design Thinking framework to develop meaningful physical activity and wellbeing solutions for your school. It also explores strategies for using data-informed decision-making to advocate for your solution, maximise opportunities for implementation success and develop evaluation approaches that determine the effectiveness of your program.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09, L10, L11
